dp.label: Economy
dp.sub: Textile mills, SME manufacturing, upper-mid income

dp.label: Competitor landscape
dp.sub: No major pan-India brand currently present

dp.label: Typical BTL channels
dp.val: RWA events, hardware stores, EMI camps

dp.label: SolarSquare brand awareness
dp.sub: No prior operations in Tamil Nadu

questions.text: From the data above, identify the top 3 factors that most strongly support a Go decision. Rank them and explain why each matters.
questions.ideal: (1) High solar irradiation (5.5–6 kWh/m²) → strong ROI, short payback. (2) High tariff (₹6.5/unit) → large savings delta. (3) No pan-India competitor → first-mover advantage. Candidate must link each to a business outcome, not just name the metric.

questions.text: Identify the single biggest risk of entering Coimbatore. What would you need to validate before committing resources?
questions.ideal: Biggest risk: near-zero brand awareness in Tamil-speaking market with local installer incumbents. Validate: (a) willingness-to-pay in target income band, (b) TANGEDCO subsidy processing speed, (c) Tamil-speaking sales/install staff availability.

questions.text: The data shows no major pan-India brand is present yet. Does that make entry more attractive or raise a concern? Argue both sides, then take a position.
questions.ideal: Attractive: first-mover pricing window, brand-building opportunity. Concern: local brands may serve market adequately. Strong candidate takes a clear position and defends it. Fence-sitters score lower.

questions.text: Final call: Go or No-Go for Coimbatore? State your decision and the one number from the data sheet that most influenced it.
questions.ideal: Go. Anchor: ₹6.5/unit tariff or 5.5–6 kWh/m² irradiation — drives payback to <5.5 yrs vs 6–7 yr national average. Red flag: citing brand awareness as the deciding number.

questions.text: Using cost, competitor price range, and subsidy data, calculate gross margin % at: ₹1.05L, ₹1.15L, and ₹1.25L. Show your working.
questions.ideal: Cost = ₹95K. At ₹1.05L: margin = 9.5%. At ₹1.15L: 17.4%. At ₹1.25L: 24.0%. Strong: notes 9.5% is thin for new market; 17–24% more sustainable.

questions.text: Coimbatore has an industrial SME base alongside residential customers. Would you charge different prices for these segments, or a single price? What's the commercial logic?
questions.ideal: Different prices. SMEs: higher consumption, faster payback, ROI-literate → pay premium. Residential: price-sensitive, subsidy-driven. Segmented pricing: residential ₹1.10–1.15L, SME 10–15% premium per kW.

questions.text: SolarSquare is a new entrant with near-zero brand awareness. How should that affect pricing — penetration, at-par, or premium? Justify with one specific reason.
questions.ideal: At-par or slight penetration (₹1.10–1.15L). Not deep penetration — signals quality risk. Not premium — brand hasn't earned it. Must tie price to a specific value prop (e.g. 5-year monitoring, faster TANGEDCO support).

questions.text: Final answer: recommended post-subsidy price for a 3 kW plant in Coimbatore. In one sentence each, explain how you've balanced growth vs profitability.
questions.ideal: ₹1.10–1.15 lakh. Growth: at-par lowers barrier for first 50–100 installs to build word-of-mouth. Profitability: 15–17% margin covers city-launch opex; breakeven in 6–9 months at 30–40 installs/month.

questions.text: List 4 metrics to evaluate BTL effectiveness — at least one leading, one lagging indicator. Define each specifically.
questions.ideal: Leading: (1) Lead-to-site-visit rate per event (target >15%). (2) Cost per qualified lead (target <₹2,000). Lagging: (3) BTL-attributed install rate within 60 days. (4) BTL CAC vs digital CAC at 90 days.

questions.text: 10 RWA events, ₹3L spend, 8 leads → site visits, 2 → installs at ₹1.15L each (~₹20K margin each). Calculate ROI. Acceptable at this stage? Why?
questions.ideal: Contribution = ₹40K. Spend = ₹3L. Net = −₹2.6L. ROI = −86.7%. Strong: ACCEPTABLE for a pilot — CAC compresses as awareness builds, LTV not captured, metric at pilot stage is lead quality trend not absolute ROI.

questions.text: What is your clearest early signal — within 3 weeks — that this channel is worth scaling? Be specific.
questions.ideal: Site-visit conversion rate from BTL leads >25% within first 3 events. Means 1 in 4 interested people schedule a visit. Secondary: at least 1 install from first 3 events. Weak: 'more leads' or 'positive feedback'.

questions.text: Customers here prefer Tamil-language communication. Name one tactical adjustment to BTL and the metric to confirm it's working.
questions.ideal: Adjustment: Tamil-speaking brand promoters, all collateral and WhatsApp follow-ups in Tamil. Metric: compare lead-to-visit conversion rate Tamil-first vs mixed-language outreach across matched events.

title: Growth & Monetisation — Manager Interview
instr: All data you need is in the Data Sheet below. State your assumptions clearly. No slides needed — write answers directly. Spend ~20 min per part. We evaluate your commercial logic, not the polish of your writing.
